subject: Anthropology
title: Nature vs. Nurture in Kinship
description: Learn Nature vs. Nurture in Kinship in one minute.

hook.text: A Trobriand father raises children he believes aren't biologically his. For him, fatherhood is care, not conception.

buildup.text: Western cultures emphasize biological connection as the basis of kinship. But many societies define it differently.

discovery.text: Schneider argued that Western kinship theory projects biological bias onto cultures that define kin through action.

twist.text: Shared food, shared labor, and co-residence create kinship in many cultures. You become family by living as family.

climax.text: The nature versus nurture debate in kinship reveals that biology is just one way to build family relationships.

punchline.text: In many cultures, you are what you do, not what you inherit.

quiz.question: What did Schneider argue about Western kinship theory?
options: It projects biological bias onto cultures that define kin through action
Biology is the only basis of kinship
